title: Wedding For Disaster

jokes.text: there are only two real commandments, and the other eight are just filler. I knew it.
jokes.setup: Reverend declaring only two commandments are real
jokes.punchline: Congregation member immediately validates this heretical claim
jokes.explanation: Clean subversion of religious authority with immediate validation from congregation

jokes.text: Oh, my God! She fainted! Let's go get smelling salts! Suckers!
jokes.setup: Woman appears to faint from religious excitement
jokes.punchline: Moe reveals it's a scam to get people to leave their spots
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe characterization - opportunistic scheme disguised as concern

jokes.text: since Mother Teresa stopped here to gas up her Vespa
jokes.setup: Comparing parson to most saintly visitor
jokes.punchline: Mother Teresa riding a Vespa scooter
jokes.explanation: Unexpected juxtaposition of saintly figure with mundane transportation

jokes.text: I'd commit arson for the parson. Clergymen can kiss my apse.
jokes.setup: Showing faith-based merchandise
jokes.punchline: Rhyming criminal act with religious figure, architectural pun
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay combining religious devotion with criminal acts

jokes.text: Surprisingly, none of them have sold. Except for this one.
jokes.setup: Showing terrible religious merchandise
jokes.punchline: Deadpan delivery that only one sold despite being awful
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about bad merchandise

jokes.text: News has never been softer.
jokes.setup: Covering trivial religious merchandise story
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment on the quality of news coverage
jokes.explanation: Concise meta-commentary on media coverage standards

jokes.text: the earthly embodiment of the elected chair of the national congress of deacons
jokes.setup: Religious fervor for visiting parson
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top bureaucratic title for religious figure
jokes.explanation: Satirizes bureaucratic nature of religious hierarchy

jokes.text: You just made my mustache tingle.
jokes.setup: Parson gives generic family values answer
jokes.punchline: News anchor's inappropriately physical response
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Kent Brockman moment - unexpectedly intimate response

jokes.text: our world spiritual headquarters in Michigan City, Indiana
jokes.setup: Expecting grand religious center
jokes.punchline: Mundane Indiana city as spiritual headquarters
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons deflation of grandeur with mundane reality

jokes.text: I love to go to church It's where I like to read / I get a day in of praying And still tee off by three
jokes.setup: Reverend singing about church devotion
jokes.punchline: Admits he reads and prioritizes golf over service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lovejoy characterization - casual about religious duties

jokes.text: He's like the pope of this thing!
jokes.setup: Helen dismisses parson as old roommate
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy's casual description of religious hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Lovejoy's casual dismissal of formal religious titles

jokes.text: That is if you had stuck with it. You asked me to quit, because I was making more than you.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy compares parson visit to Mary Kay success
jokes.punchline: Helen reveals she was more successful and he made her quit
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing marital dynamics and insecurity

jokes.text: Sounds to me like somebody put on more than just one coat. Maybe.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy brags about fresh wax on pews
jokes.punchline: Parson's subtle dig followed by Lovejoy's sheepish admission
jokes.explanation: Subtle workplace dynamic humor with religious authority

jokes.text: Walking to your office is what I like to do / Maybe while we're at it We'll peek in at your pews
jokes.setup: Parson breaking into song about mundane activities
jokes.punchline: Rhyming inspection with casual office walk
jokes.explanation: Mild character quirk of singing mundane activities

jokes.text: Helen had bought a fireplace poker from Nova Scotia. She says it shifts logs better than American pokers. But I disagree.
jokes.setup: Explaining suspicious offshore poker club charge
jokes.punchline: Elaborate explanation about Canadian fireplace poker quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd escalation with patriotic poker preference

jokes.text: In fact, I remember when she was Harold Schwarzbaum. You what? I've said too much.
jokes.setup: Parson remembering Helen fondly
jokes.punchline: Reveals Helen was previously Harold, then immediately backtracks
jokes.explanation: Shocking revelation immediately followed by comedic retreat

jokes.text: Well, I'm sure the Lord is understanding. No, He's not.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy hoping for divine understanding
jokes.punchline: Parson's flat contradiction of merciful God concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in subverting religious mercy expectation

jokes.text: That food might as well have been regurgitated by Satan himself. Regurgitated by Satan He couldn't keep it down / Oh, 666 He got sick, sick, sick / And that's how volcanoes were born
jokes.setup: Helen upset about invalid food blessing
jokes.punchline: Elaborate song about Satan's digestive issues creating volcanoes
jokes.explanation: Helen's dramatic overreaction with creative mythology about Satan's nausea

jokes.text: She still alive? I'm afraid not. 'Cos I left her ashes on the bus.
jokes.setup: Learning funeral blessing didn't count
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of losing wife's ashes on public transport
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation from invalid blessing to lost ashes

jokes.text: Maybe the next one!
jokes.setup: Learning ship blessing didn't count
jokes.punchline: Optimistic response implying expectation of ship destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sea Captain optimism about inevitable maritime disaster

jokes.text: So that's why that ghost keeps coming through the window. Afternoon. Fly away ghosty! Go back to your haunted cornfield!
jokes.setup: Learning house blessing didn't count
jokes.punchline: Casual interaction with ghost, polite dismissal to cornfield
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus moment - treats supernatural occurrence as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: We didn't order a pizza. But you forgot the pizza anyway.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy at door without pizza
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic that pizza was forgotten despite not ordering
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - criticizing failure to deliver unordered pizza

jokes.text: And I think Apu got lucky with my sister.
jokes.setup: Homer recounting wedding day events
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment about Apu and Marge's sister
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate oversharing about family members

jokes.text: We don't need a piece of paper to keep it real. Who are you calling an old lady? I need that paper quick!
jokes.setup: Homer's casual attitude about marriage certificate
jokes.punchline: Marge's priorities shift from being called old to needing legal proof
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Marge's shifting priorities

jokes.text: But in the sense someone calls you one when they're angry, yes. Why, you little bastard! That's the angry one.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining legal vs colloquial meaning of bastard
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately demonstrates with Bart, Bart identifies the usage
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-payoff with immediate demonstration of the concept

jokes.text: Yeah, instead of exhausted zombies running down the clock.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting they can pretend to be newlyweds
jokes.punchline: Bart's bleak assessment of their current marital state
jokes.explanation: Bart's surprisingly mature and cynical observation about marriage

jokes.text: Watch your back. Half a corpse coming through.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment at City Hall
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of gruesome delivery interrupting romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect romantic moment interruption with bureaucratic horror

jokes.text: That's the line to get out.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to leave unromantic venue
jokes.punchline: Even leaving requires waiting in line
jokes.explanation: Bureaucratic nightmare where even escape requires process

jokes.text: Hello. It's me, Lance Romance.
jokes.setup: Mysterious phone call to Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's terrible romantic alter ego name
jokes.explanation: Homer's predictably bad attempt at romantic mystery

jokes.text: you deserve a wedding day that, unlike our children, was planned in advance
jokes.setup: Homer's romantic speech about wedding planning
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that their children were unplanned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - romantic gesture with inappropriate honesty

jokes.text: Now back to your cold airless tomb. Ho, ho, ho.
jokes.setup: Santa congratulating engagement
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing Santa back to death, Santa's cheerful compliance
jokes.explanation: Dark subversion of Santa's jolly nature with tomb imagery

jokes.text: Marge, a woman only gets one chance at a second remarriage.
jokes.setup: Justifying expensive wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Logical paradox of 'one chance at second' remarriage
jokes.explanation: Homer's inadvertent logical paradox about remarriage opportunities

jokes.text: Well, the last time I checked, pirates weren't gay. Dude, how'd you check?
jokes.setup: Homer defending pirate tuxedo choice
jokes.punchline: Clerk's question about methodology of checking pirate sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from costume critique to inappropriate methodology question

jokes.text: Maybe we can bury the rocket ship inside the cake. And that would be a big surprise, because I'd forget about it pretty quick.
jokes.setup: Compromise suggestion about cake decoration
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware admission of poor memory
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-deprecating awareness of his forgetfulness

jokes.text: Well did he take all the blue roses with him? My father was a lovely man. He took very good care of my mother and me.
jokes.setup: Marge's aggressive demand about blue roses and dead Howard
jokes.punchline: Florist's gentle, irrelevant tribute to deceased father
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflection from aggressive customer to tender memory

jokes.text: but only one plate of butter. That'll stimulate conversation.
jokes.setup: Wedding planning details about bread service
jokes.punchline: Bridezilla logic that scarcity creates social interaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect bridezilla micromanagement with twisted social engineering

jokes.text: But there is, Marge. The first two letters. I can't believe you're ruining the second Thursday before the wedding!
jokes.setup: Marge saying there's no 'we' in wedding, Homer correcting
jokes.punchline: Marge's hyper-specific complaint about ruining pre-wedding Thursday
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay followed by Marge's absurd scheduling specificity

jokes.text: 'Cos they're not even coming to the wedding. Why not? Because they're RSVP cards arrived one day after the deadline!
jokes.setup: Homer confused about Lenny and Carl bringing dates
jokes.punchline: Marge's extreme enforcement of RSVP deadlines
jokes.explanation: Perfect bridezilla behavior - punishing friends for minor tardiness

jokes.text: More like you're a King Wrong!
jokes.setup: Homer calling her Bridezilla
jokes.punchline: Marge's attempt at comeback using King Kong reference
jokes.explanation: Marge's weak but endearing attempt at monster movie comeback

jokes.text: Not on my day, you don't.
jokes.setup: Marge discovers pimple before wedding
jokes.punchline: Aggressive confrontation with facial blemish
jokes.explanation: Bridezilla escalation to threatening inanimate objects

jokes.text: We are now eleven seconds behind schedule!
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony timing pressure
jokes.punchline: Marge's hyper-precise scheduling anxiety
jokes.explanation: Peak bridezilla micromanagement of wedding timing

jokes.text: I'm chained up like a common bicycle!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering he's been kidnapped and chained
jokes.punchline: Comparison to bicycle security rather than human bondage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer perspective - comparing kidnapping to bike theft

jokes.text: It would have to be someone who could afford a chain.
jokes.setup: Homer wondering who kidnapped him
jokes.punchline: Economic analysis of kidnapper's chain purchasing power
jokes.explanation: Homer's practical economic reasoning about criminal expenses

jokes.text: Just so you know, you're making a pretty bad first impression.
jokes.setup: Kidnapper threatening Homer's life means nothing
jokes.punchline: Homer's polite social critique of kidnapper's manners
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - treating life-threatening situation as social faux pas

jokes.text: They taste like nothing.
jokes.setup: Marge blaming herself for Homer's disappearance over spring rolls
jokes.punchline: Even in crisis, she maintains food critique
jokes.explanation: Marge's priorities - even in crisis, spring rolls get criticized

jokes.text: Plus, he loves Mom and us, yadda, yadda, yadda.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Homer wouldn't miss the wedding
jokes.punchline: Bart dismissing family love as routine exposition
jokes.explanation: Bart's meta-awareness of sitcom emotional beats as boring

jokes.text: Because he knows that if Mom and Dad don't get married, I'll never be born!
jokes.setup: Explaining Sideshow Bob's revenge motive
jokes.punchline: Bart's confused temporal logic about remarriage affecting his birth
jokes.explanation: Bart's temporal confusion about remarriage consequences

jokes.text: I'd like to hear more about it, but company policy forbids chit-chat.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions relationship status change
jokes.punchline: Corporate policy preventing human connection
jokes.explanation: Corporate dehumanization preventing basic human empathy

jokes.text: Whose side are you on?
jokes.setup: Narrator explains lollipop is made of hot sauce
jokes.punchline: Homer questioning narrator's loyalty
jokes.explanation: Homer breaking fourth wall to question unhelpful narrator

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry. Nothing's gonna happen to you, Mr. Teeny Number 7.
jokes.setup: Reassuring monkey about blimp experiment
jokes.punchline: Numbering system implies multiple predecessor deaths
jokes.explanation: Dark implication that six previous Mr. Teenys died in experiments

jokes.text: Scott Baio, the Sultan of Brunei, the former Poland secret police the Służba Bezpieczeństwa.
jokes.setup: Explaining who else could have 'S.B.' initials
jokes.punchline: Escalating from actor to royalty to foreign intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob intellectualism - escalating from pop culture to geopolitics

jokes.text: But just a hotter sauce.
jokes.setup: Homer expecting key to unlock chain
jokes.punchline: Reveal that 'key' is another torture device
jokes.explanation: Escalating torture through false hope and spicier punishment

jokes.text: They're-- Oh, you're being sarcastic. Wow! You're the smartest guy in that room! Thank you. That's very nice-- More sarcasm.
jokes.setup: Louie asking what's fun about fire ants
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony explaining sarcasm, Louie missing it repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Perfect mob dynamic with Louie's literal interpretation of sarcasm

jokes.text: You always find the one grain of sugar in the salt shaker that is I.
jokes.setup: Homer's romantic wedding vows to Marge
jokes.punchline: Tortured metaphor comparing himself to sugar contamination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer romance - accidentally insulting metaphor delivered sincerely

jokes.text: God help me, I even miss your sisters.
jokes.setup: Homer listing who he misses while kidnapped
jokes.punchline: Admitting to missing even the despised sisters-in-law
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Homer's desperation through impossible admission

jokes.text: and two sobbing trolls threw me out of a van onto our lawn.
jokes.setup: Homer describing how he was returned home
jokes.punchline: Describing Patty and Selma as 'sobbing trolls'
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual description of sisters-in-law as mythological creatures

jokes.text: Won't it dry up in a few days, and fall off in the bath?
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to remove chain from Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's biological solution to chain removal
jokes.explanation: Homer treating metal chain like organic scab or bandage

jokes.text: So maybe we could put a tanning bed in there.
jokes.setup: Having one-year lease on torture room
jokes.punchline: Practical suggestion for repurposing kidnapping facility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selma practicality - repurposing crime scene for vanity

jokes.text: Ask her. She's the brains. I'm the thing that's not the brains.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about blackmail payment
jokes.punchline: Selma's self-deprecating role definition in twin dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selma self-awareness about her role as less intelligent twin

jokes.text: We emptied our 401(k)s for this?
jokes.setup: Elaborate surprise wedding reception for Homer and Marge
jokes.punchline: Patty's regret over expensive gesture for despised brother-in-law
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty moment - regretting financial sacrifice for Homer's happiness

jokes.text: No! You're a plant now!
jokes.setup: Professor Frink creating blue roses through genetic engineering
jokes.punchline: Frink being transformed by his own creation
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink scientific backfire with transformation consequence

tmdb_overview: After Marge and Homer's marriage turns out to be invalid, they decide to re-do their vows. But when Homer goes missing, Bart and Lisa smell something funny and investigate his disappearance.
